name: storage
context: Supabase Storage — upload files, manage buckets, generate signed URLs, and transform images
info: Supabase Storage is an S3-compatible file storage service integrated with auth and RLS. Files are organized into buckets (public or private). The client SDK provides `supabase.storage.from('bucket')` with `.upload()`, `.download()`, `.getPublicUrl()`, `.createSignedUrl()`, `.remove()`, and `.list()` methods. Storage policies (similar to RLS) control access per-bucket and per-path using `auth.uid()`. Public buckets serve files without authentication. Private buckets require signed URLs or authenticated requests. Image transformations (resize, crop, format conversion) are available via URL parameters on public URLs. Storage metadata lives in the `storage.objects` and `storage.buckets` tables in Postgres.

sections.title: Bucket management
sections.body: Create buckets with `supabase.storage.createBucket()` or via SQL INSERT into `storage.buckets`. Set `public: true` for publicly accessible files (no auth needed for reads) or `public: false` for private files (require signed URLs). Configure `fileSizeLimit` and `allowedMimeTypes` to restrict uploads.

sections.title: Upload and download
sections.body: Upload with `.upload(path, file, options)`. Path becomes the object key within the bucket. Use `upsert: true` to overwrite existing files. Set `contentType` explicitly for non-browser environments. Download with `.download(path)` which returns a Blob. For server-side, convert to Buffer with `Buffer.from(await blob.arrayBuffer())`.

sections.title: Public and signed URLs
sections.body: Public buckets: use `getPublicUrl(path)` for a permanent URL. Private buckets: use `createSignedUrl(path, expiresIn)` for a time-limited URL. Signed URLs can include image transforms. Use `createSignedUrls()` (plural) to generate multiple URLs in one call. Signed URLs contain a JWT token in the query string.

sections.title: Storage policies
sections.body: Storage policies are RLS policies on `storage.objects`. Use `bucket_id`, `storage.foldername(name)`, `storage.filename(name)`, and `auth.uid()` in conditions. Common pattern: restrict uploads/reads to `<user_id>/*` paths using `(storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text`. Policies apply to all operations: SELECT (read), INSERT (upload), UPDATE (overwrite), DELETE.

sections.title: Image transformations
sections.body: Transform images on-the-fly with `width`, `height`, `resize` (cover/contain/fill), `quality` (1-100), and `format` (origin/avif). Pass transforms to `getPublicUrl()` or `createSignedUrl()` in the `transform` option. Transforms are cached at the CDN edge. Only works with image/* content types.
